gaps: seed_pods=0: The elongated cylindrical seed capsules are the plant's key self-sowing feature (prominently discussed in care/seasons) and there is no pod shot. Need a summer/fall shot of the ribbed capsules splitting open or standing ripe on the plant.

whole_plant=1: su-habit is a wide meadow sweep — the individual plant's sprawling mound/taproot form is not shown as a standalone. su-foliage incidentally shows a single plant, but a dedicated clean single-plant habit shot (8–15 in mound in open ground) would reach 2.
NOTE: seasonal=NA is correct — frost-tender summer annual, no multi-season interest.

thumb_framing=1: su-habit-t.jpg thumbnail is cluttered — the clump is nearly indistinguishable from surrounding prairie grasses; the subject is lost. A cleaner single-clump summer shot with clear blue-green colour and separation from background would reach 2. The fa-habit and wi-habit thumbs are excellent; the su-habit is the weak link.

seed_pods=0: Buff/round seed capsules ripening Jul–Aug are mentioned as a seasonal feature in the seasons field ('buff seed capsules ripen Jul–Aug') and are notable for the plant's self-sowing story. No pod shot exists. Need a late-summer shot of the rounded capsules on the wiry stems.

seasonal=1: All 4 shots are summer. The plant has a clear fall/seed-capsule stage (Jul–Aug buff capsules) and the blue-green stems persist into fall. A late-summer or fall shot of seed capsules would cover a second season and lift this to 2.

thumb_framing=1: su-bloom-t.jpg has the flowers slightly clipped at the edges and the subjects (wiry stems + flowers) are small relative to the frame. The image is recognisable but less than ideal — re-thumbnailing or replacing with a tighter composition would reach 2.

thumb_framing=0: su-flower-t.jpg has the top of the yellow flower cup cut off; the petals are clipped at the top edge of the thumbnail, making the key ornamental feature (the large papery cup) incomplete. This needs re-thumbnailing from the full image or replacement with a properly framed flower shot.

flowers=1: su-flower is an extreme close-up that clips the flower at the top — a properly framed full cup (3–4 in across, all four petals visible) is needed. The current shot does not show the complete flower.
front_facing=1: su-flower shows the flower but the crop is too tight and cuts off petals — not a complete face-on view. A full-face shot of the open cup (ideally in the evening light) would reach 2.

bloom_in_context=1: Only the su-flower (clipped close-up) and su-habit (one flower in a cluttered plant view) exist. For a plant whose 3–4 in blooms are described as 'among the showiest of any xeric perennial,' a proper sprawling-mound-in-bloom shot (showing the plant carpeted with flowers) is needed to reach 2.

whole_plant=1: su-habit shows the sprawling mound with lance-shaped leaves and one flower, but the surrounding vegetation is cluttered and the plant boundary is not clearly defined. A cleaner shot of the low spreading mound in open ground would reach 2.

whole_plant=0: su-habit-t.jpg shows a garden border at a distance where the knautia plant is tiny and in the background — completely unreadable as a plant portrait. Need a clear shot of a knautia clump (24–32 in, wiry branching stems, airy open form) in full view.

foliage=0: su-foliage-t.jpg is incorrectly captioned as knautia foliage but shows a dark mass of purple/crimson flowers (not knautia's grey-green pinnate leaves) in a crowded border — leaf shape and texture are completely unreadable. Need a shot clearly showing the grey-green pinnate/lobed basal and stem foliage.
thumb_framing=0: su-habit-t.jpg thumbnail shows the plant as a tiny distant element — the subject is essentially lost. The foliage thumbnail is also confusing/unreadable. Both shots need replacement.

bloom_in_context=1: su-bloom shows one strong pincushion head with a few others visible in background — partial context. A wider shot of multiple stems weaving through a border (the plant's signature 'airy drift' look) is needed for score 2.

seasonal=1: All shots are summer. Knautia blooms July–September (into October with deadheading) and has a persistent basal rosette through fall — a fall shot showing late-season bloom or the rosette after stems die back would add the second season.

foliage=1: su-foliage-t.jpg shows a single spent flower head from the side/above with foliage below it — the broad ovate toothed leaves are visible but are secondary to the flower. A dedicated foliage shot (close-up of the aromatic square-stemmed leaves, ideally showing the toothed margins and hairy texture) would reach 2.

seasonal=1: The plant has only summer shots and one fall/seedhead shot. The seasons field notes 'seed heads & stems hold into winter' — a winter shot of the standing dried stems and seedheads in cold/snow conditions (matching the plant's stated winter structure) would lift this to 2.

foliage=1: su-flower shows foliage behind the flowers and su-habit shows it in the mass bloom context, but leaf shape/texture is not clearly readable — the foliage is secondary and blurred. A close shot of the broad ovate toothed mint-family leaves (with square stems visible) would reach 2.

seed_pods=1: wi-seedhead is a tight crop of a single dried seedhead — the 'seed heads standing through winter feeding goldfinches' story is only partially told. A wider shot showing multiple seedheads on the standing stems in a fall/winter landscape would reach 2.

seasonal=1: Summer (flower) and winter (seedhead) are covered but fall is missing — the transition from spent bloom to dried seedhead formation (September–October) is a distinct season that is not covered. A fall shot of the plant with browning stems and fresh seedheads forming would complete the three-season story.

whole_plant=1: sp-bloom shows canes with multiple flowers and foliage but it is a fairly close shot — the overall shrub form (2–6 ft multistemmed, running/thicket-forming habit) is not visible. A wider shot showing the full shrub silhouette or a thicket would reach 2.

thumb_framing=1: wi-hip-t.jpg is a very tight crop of a single shriveled hip with a strongly blurred background — the tiny hip is centered but barely visible against the bokeh. A slightly wider composition showing the hip on the cane in winter context would be clearer and more informative.

front_facing=1: The tubular pendant flowers are shown from below/side in su-flower and su-habit — the tube openings and stamens are partially visible but never shown face-on from below (which is the 'face' of a tubular honeysuckle). A shot looking up into the whorl from directly below, or a close-up showing the open tube ends and exserted stamens clearly, would reach 2.

seed_pods=1: 'Occasional small orange-red berries' are mentioned in the seasons field — there is no berry shot. While berries are described as occasional, they are a listed seasonal feature. Likely achievable from iNat (fruiting phenology query); if not available under an open license, note as 'likely capped (no CC berry shot for this cultivar)'.

thumb_framing=1: su-habit-t.jpg has an oversized fused leaf dominating the upper half of the frame with the pendant flower cluster hanging below — unusual composition where the flower is pushed toward the bottom edge. The flower cluster is the feature but it is subordinated to the leaf. A reframe with the flower cluster centered would reach 2.

seasonal=1: All 4 shots are summer. The plant blooms from June into October on the Front Range ('one of the longest bloom windows of any hardy vine') — a fall shot showing the lighter rebloom, autumn foliage, or the orange-red berries would add a meaningful second season.

whole_plant=1: su-habit shows branching stems with flowers at several stages — does not read as a clear full-plant form (stems trail/lean, not an upright whole-plant silhouette); add or replace with a shot that clearly shows the full airy plant height and branching habit.

flowers=1 / front_facing=1: su-flower shows the main focal daisy slightly side-angled with ray petals partially reflexed; source a fully face-on shot with flat/horizontal rays showing the diagnostic hollow gold cone (the ID character that distinguishes it from scentless chamomile).

foliage=1: sp-foliage is dominated by the blue flowers; the tiny dark-green leaves are in the background and not the clear subject. Add a dedicated shot of the dense dark evergreen mat without flowers — ideally showing the small leaf shape and texture in a close but readable frame (fall or winter would also fill the seasonal gap simultaneously).

thumb_framing=1: sp-bloom thumbnail is a portrait-orientation crop (tall/narrow) that creates wasted space on a 3:2 card; the cobalt mat reads small and off-center. Replace with a landscape-oriented mass-bloom image or re-run rethumb.py on a better-framed source image.

seasonal=1: winter=true, plant described as 'reliably evergreen dark green mat year-round' with potential purplish winter tints. Only spring shots present. Add a fall or winter shot showing the mat's year-round evergreen character (the dark foliage with possible purple cast) — this would also address the foliage gap.

whole_plant=1: sp-habit (the 'whole plant in shaded woodland' shot) shows a plant with WHITE flowers, not the characteristic blue of Brunnera macrophylla — this appears to be either the plain green-leaved species photographed before full colour development, or a misidentified plant. The shot is useful for overall form but the white flower colour is confusing and diverges from every other shot in the reel. Replace with a clearly blue-flowered whole-plant habit shot.

bloom_in_context=1: sp-bloom provides an excellent close-up spray of blue flowers, and sp-habit provides a mass context shot — however, the sp-habit's white flowers make it an unreliable 'mass bloom' reference for this plant. Once sp-habit is replaced with a correctly blue-flowered mass shot, bloom_in_context will reach 2.

whole_plant=1: su-habit shows a single partially cropped spike with foliage (basal leaves visible but plant cropped at top and bottom); su-bloom shows a colony of spires but is a mid-distance shot where no individual plant form is readable. Add a shot showing a complete plant — upright spires plus the full basal rosette of palmate leaves — ideally one plant clearly separated from its neighbours to read the habit.

whole_plant=0: The shot labelled sp-habit shows only 2–3 close-up flowers overhead — there is NO whole-plant habit shot in the reel. Add a shot showing the full spreading mound of silver-woolly stems and multiple flowers, ideally from a slight distance to show the clumping/spreading growth form and overall plant silhouette.

seasonal=1: Plant blooms May–Jul with deadheading potentially extending to fall; silver-woolly foliage is present spring and fall; all current shots are spring. Add a fall shot showing the plant reblooming or with spent seedhead texture, OR a summer shot after the main flush showing the silvery-grey foliage mound — the fall rebloom would be the most useful seasonal addition.

foliage=0: Neither shot shows readable leaf shape or texture — the habit shot has blooms dominant and leaves are secondary blur. Shasta daisy has distinctive lance-shaped toothed dark-green basal leaves; add a shot where the foliage is clearly readable (e.g. the basal rosette before or after bloom, or a mid-summer foliage shot after deadheading).

whole_plant=1: su-close shows a full flowering clump but the composition is dense/cluttered (multiple stems overlapping, compressed depth-of-field); no shot gives a clear silhouette of the mound form. Add a shot showing the distinct low silvery mound topped with flat golden corymbs from a slight distance, showing the full plant height and spread.

seasonal=0: Plant has silver-grey ferny foliage spring through fall, semi-evergreen in mild winters, and seedheads that add winter texture. Only summer bloom shots present. Add at minimum a spring foliage shot (showing the silver ferny rosette before bloom) and ideally a fall/winter shot showing dried seedheads on the persistent basal foliage — this is achievable from iNaturalist or GBIF with --month 9,10,11 or --phenology fruiting.

seasonal=1: winter=true — plant described as 'Evergreen silver-gray mound year-round... fragrant stems persist into winter.' Only summer shots present. Add a winter or late-fall shot showing the persistent grey mound and dried stems, or the silver foliage through frost/snow — this is the one gap preventing excellent. Likely achievable from iNaturalist with --month 11,12,1,2 (global) since this is a widespread cultivated plant.

foliage=0: No shot shows the compound leaves of clematis — a distinctive feature (opposite, pinnate, leaf-stalks used for twining). Add a shot of the foliage, ideally showing the twining mechanism and leaf detail.

seed_pods=0: The fluffy silver seedheads are explicitly called out in the plant description ('wispy silver-grey seedheads that persist into fall') and in the care/bloom notes. This is a notable seasonal feature and a key garden-interest point — no shot present. Add a fall shot of the fluffy seedhead clusters (iNaturalist --phenology fruiting --month 9,10,11 for Clematis × jackmanii or parent species, global).
seasonal=0: Plant has summer bloom + notable fall/winter fluffy seedheads. Only summer shots present. The seedhead shot above would address this — add fall (seedheads) to reach seasonal=2.

whole_plant=1: su-foliage shows a drift of cosmos from mid-distance but no single plant with full stem-to-soil habit readable; add a clear whole-plant shot showing airy branching form and height in context

foliage=1: the 'foliage' shot (su-foliage-t) is actually a blooming drift (the bloom-in-context shot), not a dedicated leaf-texture shot; the iconic thread-fine ferny leaves are barely legible — add a close shot clearly showing the finely dissected pinnate foliage

front_facing=1: su-flower-t (white bloom) is good and face-on; su-bloom-t (magenta) is slightly top-down, petals show from above rather than straight face-on — replace or supplement with a fully face-on close-up

bloom_in_context=1: su-foliage-t is the mass-of-bloom shot (actually a nice drift) but there is no single crisp face-on close-up of the flower that also reads well in the thumb — su-flower-t is the close-up but the thumb is badly framed (bloom cut off at top, mostly grass stems); add or replace with a close-up that is centered in the 720×480 thumb

thumb_framing=1: su-flower-t thumbnail is severely mis-framed — the white cosmos bloom is cropped off at the top edge, and the frame is dominated by blurry grass stems; the subject is not centered or clearly visible at card size — re-source or rethumb with a better crop

foliage=1: both shots are taken during full bloom — the succulent leaves are visible but always secondary behind the flowers; need a shot where the glaucous succulent mat is the clear subject (e.g. late season or non-peak timing), showing the distinctive cylindrical leaf texture

seasonal=1: winter is listed as notable (winter=true, seasons mention year-round evergreen mat); the reel has only summer shots — add a winter shot of the evergreen succulent mat in a dormant/cold state to show year-round interest; likely capped if no CC winter shot of D. cooperi exists, but worth checking iNat with --month 11,12,1,2

seasonal=1: winter=true and the blurb explicitly highlights year-round glossy dark evergreen foliage through the Front Range winter, yet the reel has only spring bloom shots — a winter foliage shot would confirm the claim and complete the year; likely achievable since candytuft is widely photographed in winter gardens; query iNat with --month 12,1,2 or Commons for a winter mat shot

seasonal=1: seasons field notes 'bronze fall' color and the care text mentions fall foliage yellowing and the copper/bronze spring emergence; the reel has four spring shots with no fall representation — a fall foliage shot (bronzing leaves) would complete the seasonal story; likely achievable via iNat --month 9,10

whole_plant=1: sp-bloom-t shows one flower from above with surrounding foliage — the plant's overall habit (the clumping basal rosette + tall scapes) is not clearly shown; the 'habit' shot (sp-bloom) is taken from above looking down, which obscures the vertical structure — add a side-on shot showing the full clump with flowers at height

front_facing=1: sp-flower-t thumbnail frames the bloom from a slightly oblique/elevated angle (the full image is more face-on but the thumbnail crop cuts the top third of the flower off and emphasizes the interior at a tilted angle); sp-bloom-t shows the flower from above — neither is a clean face-on shot at eye-level; need a true face-on shot centered in the frame

thumb_framing=1: sp-flower-t thumbnail has the top of the red bloom cropped off at the upper edge; the main feature (the full corolla and stamens) is not centered or fully visible at card size — re-crop or re-source

seasonal=1: the plant has a distinctive fall re-emergence (new basal foliage returns in September) and the care text covers this behavior; the reel has spring bloom + summer pod but no fall shot of the returning basal foliage — add a fall foliage shot; note that the foliage is also interesting in the bristly-hairy spring state (well covered) so fall is the missing season

seed_pods=1: the care text mentions seed pods that split explosively and are notable for self-sowing naturalization; the reel has no seed pod / ripening pod shot — add a shot of the brown/tan ripe pods or the explosive split pods in late summer (iNat --phenology fruiting or --month 8,9)

foliage=1: the 'su-foliage' shot shows summer seed capsules (spent panicles in bud/capsule form) with some heart-shaped leaves in background — the leaf shape is readable but the focus is on the fruit/capsule clusters, not on clean foliage; the heart-shaped leaves that distinguish lilac are only secondary in this shot — add a dedicated shot of the characteristic large heart-shaped leaves cleanly filling the frame

seasonal=1: the lilac has three distinct seasons of interest — spring bloom (covered), summer foliage (partially covered), and bare woody winter frame / sucker structure (not covered); the seasons field describes 'bare woody frame in winter' but no winter shot exists; a winter shot showing the multi-stem structure would complete the year; likely achievable via iNat or Commons --month 12,1,2

whole_plant=1: su-habit-t shows a top-down aerial view of the plant covered in spent/drying mauve bracts with no visible live violet flowers and no sense of the mounded form from the side — the plant's characteristic 2–3 ft dome silhouette is not readable; replace with a side-on or three-quarter view showing the full rounded mound in bloom

flowers=1: none of the four shots shows crisp, fully open fresh violet flowers prominently; su-flowers-t shows the plant with a handful of tiny blue flower tips barely visible amid dominant dry mauve bracts; su-flower-t shows the bract clusters and a few partially open flowers but the violet blooms are small and partially obscured — need a shot where fresh, fully open violet flowers are the prominent subject

front_facing=1: no shot shows the violet tubular flowers face-on with petals clearly readable; the available shots are distant, from above, or show the flowers at an angle partly hidden by bracts — need a face-on close-up of fresh open flowers

bloom_in_context=1: su-habit-t is the massed plant but it shows the plant largely in a dried/spent bract state rather than in peak bloom; su-flowers-t is a partial close-up of bracts with token flowers; neither provides both a quality close-up of fresh flowers AND a shot of the mounded plant in peak bloom — need both

seasonal=1: winter=true and the blurb highlights 'lasting mauve bracts' and 'winter structure'; the reel has only summer-labeled shots — add a shot of the persistent bract/winter structure to show the claimed winter interest; this is a defining feature of the plant

foliage=0: neither shot includes a readable foliage image — the flower shot is a tight cluster of blooms with leaves as pure background, and the habit shot is distant architecture-scale showing the pergola tunnel with tiny flowers; the rose's compound leaves (leaflets, stipules) are never the subject and are not legible in either shot — add a dedicated foliage shot showing leaf shape, texture, and compound structure

seed_pods=1: the seasons field and care text mention hips feeding birds and some varieties producing hips in fall; no hip shot exists — add a fall shot of the small hips; note that not all rambler cultivars produce prominent hips (Paul's Himalayan Musk does produce small hips), so this is partially achievable; query iNat --phenology fruiting or Commons

seasonal=1: the plant has a winter interest in arching cane structure (mentioned in plant.json seasons: 'some hips & arching canes in winter') and a fall interest in hips; the reel has only summer shots — add at minimum a fall hip shot and ideally a winter cane/structure shot to complete the seasonal story

whole_plant=1: su-habit shows a single spike cropped tight with a golden-grass background — good for showing the flower-spike height but the base of the plant is cut off; need a shot showing full plant stature from ground up (stem, base foliage, complete spike), summer

foliage=1: su-foliage shows the finely divided ferny leaves in context but they are secondary to the flowers and somewhat small in frame; a dedicated shot of the feathery foliage closer up would score 2, summer

front_facing=1: su-habit and su-bloom both show florets at a slight angle or side view; su-flower (the dedicated close-up) shows florets from the front but many are darkly backlit and partially side-on; only some florets are truly face-on; need a shot with multiple florets clearly face-on in good light

bloom_in_context=1: su-bloom and su-foliage both show multiple plants/spikes together but neither is a true 'drift' or massed planting shot that shows how larkspur reads in a garden context — su-foliage has spikes scattered across the frame but mixed with other species; need a massed-drift shot of larkspur only, summer

seed_pods=1: su-habit incidentally shows a few maturing pods on the side stem but pods are small and not the subject; larkspur's elongated seed pods are a notable feature mentioned in the blurb; need a dedicated shot of mature seed pods, summer

foliage=1: sp-flower is a very tight macro of the urn-shaped flowers; the glossy rounded leaves are visible but secondary and partially out of frame; su-habit shows leaves well in context of the mat but they are small and distant; fa-pods and fa-berries show leaves alongside berries but the foliage itself is not the subject; no shot where leaf shape, gloss, and texture are the clear focus; need a close foliage shot showing the distinctive rounded, glossy dark-green leaves, ideally with the bronze-red winter coloring, fall or winter

seasonal=1: reel covers spring (flowers), summer (habit), fall (berries×2) — the plant has notable winter interest (glossy evergreen leaves turn bronze-red in cold, mentioned in blurb and seasons field; winter=true); no winter shot exists; need a winter shot showing the bronze-red foliage or the mat under/after snow, winter

whole_plant=1: sp-bloom shows a few plants in alpine meadow context and su-habit shows flowers + buds + some foliage, but neither gives a clean read of the whole plant's basal-rosette-to-stem-to-flower architecture; the best candidate (sp-bloom) is a mid-range shot, but foliage at the base is obscured; need a shot that shows the complete plant from the ferny basal mound up through the long flowering stems, spring or early summer

foliage=1: su-pods (actually a spur/flower shot, mislabeled) and su-habit both show the lacy foliage but it is always secondary to the flowers and blurred at this focus distance; need a dedicated shot of the distinctive ferny, three-lobed foliage where leaf shape and texture are clearly readable, summer

bloom_in_context=1: sp-bloom shows a handful of plants in a high-alpine meadow — this is the context shot, but plants are mid-range with sky/rock background dominating; there is no shot of a garden drift or mass planting; the single-flower close-up (su-flower) is excellent; a massed drift in-bloom shot would score 2, spring or early summer

seed_pods=1: su-pods is actually a spur/flower detail shot (mislabeled 'pods' in the filename but the caption says 'spur detail' and the image confirms this); columbine seed pods are described as notable (CLAUDE.md lists columbine pods explicitly); no actual seed-pod shot exists; need a shot of the upright seed follicles after bloom, summer

foliage=1: su-foliage shows a close-up of the opposite lance-shaped leaves on an upright stem — good concept, but the shot is poorly lit (dark, backlit), the background is very busy and distracting, and the leaves appear slightly out of focus; leaf shape is readable but the shot quality is weak; replace with a better-lit, cleaner foliage shot, summer

foliage=1: all four shots are flower-focused; su-close (the thumbnail crop of su-close.jpg) shows the lower stem with some visible leaves, but they are partially in focus and secondary; su-habit is the best foliage context but leaves are small/distant; su-flower (blue iNat spike) shows leaf rosette at base but it is very small in frame; no shot clearly showing the basal leaf rosette or the stem leaves (toothed/ovate) in a readable way; need a dedicated foliage shot, summer

front_facing=1: su-close.jpg (the 'Icicle' spike) shows florets opening bottom-up on the spike — but this is a profile/side view of the spike, not individual florets seen face-on; the small Veronica flowers do open into a star but the spike itself is always shown in profile; su-flower (blue) similarly shows a vertical spike in profile; face-on framing of individual starry florets is theoretically achievable but currently none of the shots show the floret face clearly; this is a difficult shot type for a spike flower; need a close-up of individual florets face-on (front view down the spike axis), summer

seed_pods=1: no shot of the ripe blue-purple grape-like berry clusters exists; sp-close shows yellow flower buds with some leaves but no berries; wi-habit shows the bronze-red winter foliage mat; the seasonal reel is missing the fall berry shot entirely — blue-purple berries ripening Sep–Oct are a key identifier and garden feature (mentioned prominently in blurb and seasons); need a fall shot of ripened dusty-blue berry clusters on the plant, fall

thumb_framing=1: wi-habit-t.jpg is a heavily colour-saturated overhead shot with a fallen yellow leaf dominating the centre — the leaf is the visual subject, not the plant; the creeping mat is seen but the composition reads as 'autumn leaf on red carpet' not 'this is how Berberis repens looks in winter'; the image is also flagged as possibly not Berberis repens (the leaf shape in wi-habit is very small-rounded, more consistent with Cotoneaster horizontalis or a cotoneaster cultivar than with Berberis repens which has larger, distinctly holly-like leaflets); sp-structure-t.jpg is well-framed but shows the mat as very small relative to the large boulders; need a better winter/fall habit shot that clearly shows the bronze-red holly-like foliage of Berberis repens

foliage=1: su-foliage-t.jpg shows the needle-fine foliage close up — good concept — but the shot is taken directly above the plant (top-down), the framing shows rock/debris background, and a stem intrudes across the top; the foliage detail is somewhat readable but the shot angle makes it hard to understand the plant's texture and the needles appear flattened; su-habit-t.jpg shows foliage in context but it is secondary; need a cleaner side-on close-up of the needle-fine evergreen foliage where the texture and arrangement are clearly readable, summer

front_facing=1: tubular/pendulous penstemon flowers are inherently side-facing, so a strict face-on view is architecturally NA — but the current shots show only top-down or side-and-above angles; the closest to front-facing is su-flower (hand-held) which is a side view of the tube opening; a shot showing the flared tube opening and the five-lobed mouth from the front would score 2; alternatively, given the tubular nature of this flower, front_facing could be argued NA; scored 1 because tube opening detail is achievable

bloom_in_context=1: su-flower and su-bloom are both single-flower close-ups (one on a wiry stem, one hand-held); there is no massed-plant-in-bloom shot — need a mass-blooming plant or drift showing the 'cheerful golden daisy' impact described in the blurb, summer

thumb_framing=1: su-bloom thumbnail shows a hand gripping the stem with fingers across the lower third; su-foliage thumbnail shows fingers/hand in the lower-left holding the stem in rocky gravel — both are hand-dominated and off-centre; need clean unobstructed shots

whole_plant=1: sp-foliage shows a naturalized colony in lawn with multiple plants visible, giving context — but no shot that clearly shows the low 4–6 in individual plant habit (single tuber clump, deeply lobed foliage, compact form); the colony shot partially addresses this but a single-plant or small clump clearly framed would reach 2, spring

front_facing=1: sp-flower (full image) is a top-down/slightly overhead view of one open flower plus a side-view closed bud — not face-on; sp-bloom shows a good face-on blue flower but from a slight angle with the flower edge partially cropped; neither is a fully clean face-on shot — need a direct face-on shot of the open daisy bloom clearly showing the yellow-green centre, spring

whole_plant=1: sp-bloom shows a colony of many plants on a hillside — the low fuzzy-stemmed habit is partially readable but it is mid-distance and soft; no crisp single-plant shot showing the full clumping form with fuzzy stems emerging from the crown — need a close-up habit shot of one clump in bloom, spring

flowers=1: sp-flower shows a heavily back-lit, heavily shaggy/ruffled multi-petaled form that is past its prime or an unusual cultivar — this does not cleanly show the typical open lavender cup with golden stamens that defines the species; sp-foliage shows still-closed purple buds — need a crisp open cup in direct light, spring

front_facing=0: sp-flower is angled-above with the bloom appearing heavily shaggy and back-lit — no clear face-on view of the open cup; sp-bloom is a hillside colony at ground-level distance — cups are not face-on; sp-foliage shows closed buds side-on — no shot in the reel gives a face-on view of the open lavender cup with golden stamens; need a direct face-on close-up, spring

bloom_in_context=1: sp-bloom is a colony mass shot (present, scores the mass-context); but the single-flower close-up (sp-flower) is weak (back-lit, shaggy) — the close-up half of the pair needs replacing with a crisp face-on cup, spring

seed_pods=1: sp-seedhead thumbnail shows seedheads tiny and surrounded by dense dead straw/grass, making the silky plumes nearly unreadable at card thumbnail size; the full image is better but the plumes are still embedded in debris — need a cleaner shot of the shimmering silver-plume seedheads against a lighter background, spring/early summer
thumb_framing=1: sp-seedhead thumbnail has the seedheads small and obscured by dry grass litter; sp-flower thumbnail has the bloom centred but is soft/back-lit making it visually weak at card size

seasonal=1: all 4 shots are spring; the plant has attractive ferny foliage through summer before dying back in midsummer heat — a summer foliage shot would complete the two-season story (spring bloom + summer foliage). Capped at 1 if no suitable CC summer foliage shot; iNat --phenology no-evidence or general observation in summer should surface foliage shots

whole_plant=1: the full su-habit image is actually a close-up of several flower clusters with foliage visible in the background — it does not show the trailing mat form and how the plant spreads as a groundcover; su-bloom thumbnail shows a distant woodland clearing with scattered verbena as small dots (individual plants unreadable) — need a shot that clearly shows the trailing-stem mat and the plant's low spreading form, summer

thumb_framing=1: su-bloom thumbnail frames the scene as a wide woodland landscape with verbena as tiny purple specks at a distance — the subject is not centered or legible at 720×480 card size; su-foliage thumbnail has the flower cluster pushed to the left edge with the foliage dominant on the right and the flower partially cropped — need a re-shot habit image and cleaner flower-in-context shot

whole_plant=1: su-habit shows a small/young shrub growing along a stone wall — it shows branching structure and flowers but is too small/immature to convey the 6–9 ft mature vase-shaped arching habit; the full image is a young wall-trained specimen — need a shot of a mature full-size shrub in bloom showing the sweeping arching form, summer

bloom_in_context=1: all three shots are close-range branch/flower shots; there is no shot of the full shrub billowing in bloom ('clusters of pure-white flowers blanket the arching branches') — only branch/stem close-ups exist; need a full-shrub-in-bloom shot at a distance that shows the mass of white flowers over the whole plant, summer

seasonal=1: all 3 shots are summer bloom season; the plant has noted yellow fall color and arching winter stem structure — need a fall color or winter structure shot to reach 2. Fall color (iNat --month 9,10,11) or winter stems shot would complete the seasonal story, fall or winter

front_facing=1: su-flowers shows the flowering spike from the side at close range; the tiny tubular blue flowers are visible but not face-on; fa-spikes is a mid-distance spike shot against landscape — also not face-on. The small tubular flower form makes strict face-on photography difficult; likely capped at 1 for front_facing given the flower architecture (tiny 5mm tubular blooms on tall spikes); a very close macro of a few open tube flowers from front would reach 2 but no such shot exists, summer

seasonal=1: su-habit and su-flowers are both summer bloom; fa-spikes is tagged fall but shows the plant still in full purple bloom (not the silver post-bloom or winter structure the plant is noted for, winter=true); the standing silver winter stems — explicitly described as a key winter interest feature — are not covered; need a winter shot showing the silver stems standing in frost or snow, winter (iNat or GBIF --month 12,1,2,3)
seed_pods=0: add a summer shot of the round seed capsules on spent spikes — Antirrhinum seed capsules are a distinctive feature; iNat --phenology fruiting

foliage=1: sp-foliage labeled shot primarily shows orange flower clusters with small secondary leaves; add or replace with a shot where the narrow lanceolate gray-green leaves are legible — iNat spring vegetative or basal-rosette observation

flowers=1, front_facing=1: sp-flowers shows racemes on a branch at mid-distance; individual chokecherry flowers are tiny but a closer shot of a raceme showing the individual white blossoms would score 2; iNat spring Colorado --phenology flowering

whole_plant=1: su-habit-t.jpg is a tight close-up on a single stem-top rather than the full upright plant (1–3 ft tall, self-seeding colony look). Need a summer shot stepping back to show the complete plant or a drift of several plants, capturing the colony/prairie-patch habit.

bloom_in_context=1: su-flower-t.jpg is an excellent close-up of the white-bracted bracts; su-foliage is a single-stem shot not a garden mass. Missing a wide-angle summer shot of multiple plants together showing the frosted-white colony display — the 'snow on the mountain' effect you only read at distance.

whole_plant=1: su-habit-t.jpg (labeled 'habit') shows a single plant fallen/cropped sideways against dead grass — the subject is cut off at the left edge and the plant form is not readable. su-bloom (full) shows two flowers at the top of tall bare stems in weedy meadow grass, also lacking the typical mounding garden form. Need a clear summer shot of the full plant showing its low mounding clump form.

foliage=1: su-foliage-t.jpg shows the basal rosette clearly but it's a top-down shot of just a tiny seedling/rosette on bare soil — the plant is tiny relative to the frame and the leaf texture is partially shadowed. The hairy lance-shaped leaves need a cleaner, better-lit shot where leaf texture is fully readable.

bloom_in_context=1: Only one of the two required elements is clearly met. su-flower is an excellent single face-on close-up. su-bloom is intended as the 'mass in bloom' shot but the thumbnail shows two drooping/side-on flowers among tall grass — not a mass/drift showing how blanket flower reads in a garden or meadow. Need a true mass-bloom shot: multiple plants flowering together.

thumb_framing=1: su-bloom-t.jpg has the two flowers off-center/top and the main subject partly out of frame on the left in the thumbnail crop. su-habit-t.jpg has the plant cut off at the left edge of the thumbnail.

seasonal=1: Five shots all labeled 'summer' except fa-seedhead (fall). The fall shot is excellent (seedheads + late flowers). Missing a spring shot showing the plant's early-season basal rosette emerging, which is useful context for a perennial that dies back and re-sprouts. The plant has a documented early-spring basal-growth phase worth showing.

thumb_framing=1: su-foliage-t.jpg — the smart-crop landed on top-of-frame, leaving a large bare-soil patch dominating the lower half and pushing the subject (flower + foliage) into the top third. The flower is partially cut off at top of the thumbnail. A re-crop or replacement shot (foliage + flower, filling the frame) would fix this.
thumb_framing note: su-flower-t.jpg shows a slightly angled/side bloom rather than fully face-on in the thumbnail, though the full image is clear enough — minor, not penalized separately.

seasonal=1: Has spring (sp-habit, sp-flower, sp-bloom) and summer (su-foliage) shots. The blurb/seasons field says 'Yellow-green flowerheads; soft mounded foliage' with no mention of notable fall or winter interest, and winter=false. However the plant is a rhizomatous perennial that dies back entirely — a fall shot showing the foliage turning/yellowing before dieback and/or the plant's dormant mat structure could meaningfully show its seasonal cycle. The CLAUDE.md rubric notes NA only if the plant 'truly has one season of interest'; this plant's summer blue-green foliage phase is distinct from its spring bloom phase, already covered. Fall dieback context is a plausible third stage but no strong visual differentiation is documented. Capped at 1 since only two seasons shown; likely capped: limited CC fall shots of *Euphorbia brachycera* exist on iNat.

foliage=1: su-foliage-t.jpg / su-foliage.jpg is a heavily backlit, soft-focus shot where the individual flowers are out-of-focus and the foliage is barely readable — it reads mainly as color/shape impression rather than showing the gray-green aromatic leaves and wrinkled leaf texture that distinguish this salvia. Need a cleaner, sharper shot where the distinctive gray-green foliage is the primary subject, leaf surface visible.

seasonal=1: All three shots are summer. The plant is described as having 'aromatic gray-green foliage' that 'often stays semi-evergreen under snow' with a low rosette through fall/winter. A fall or late-autumn shot showing the semi-evergreen basal rosette, or a spring shot showing early emergence before bloom (the plant's reblooming pattern and cut-back recovery), would meaningfully extend the seasonal story. The deadhead-to-rebloom behavior documented in care (late June cut-back → August–September second flush) is also worth a second-bloom shot to show the rebloom habit.

foliage=1: All four shots show the plant in full bloom where tiny leaves are small and secondary to the flower mass — the heath-like (ericoides = 'like heath') fine wiry leaves are part of the plant's ID character but no shot isolates or foregrounds them. Need a summer shot (pre-bloom, July–August) where the wiry stems with their tiny linear leaves are the subject, showing the characteristic 'heath-like' fine texture.

seed_pods=1: The seasons field explicitly states 'persistent feathery seedheads well into winter' and winter=true. fa-bloom-t.jpg and fa-habit2-t.jpg show some transitional seedheads but none of the shots clearly feature the mature feathery/fluffy seedhead stage that provides winter bird-food and visual interest. Need a late-fall or winter shot of the dried feathery seedheads on wiry stems.

seasonal=1: All four shots are fall-only. The plant has documented winter interest (feathery seedheads, winter=true). Need a winter shot showing the persistent seedhead structure on bare wiry stems — this is an explicitly called-out feature of the plant.

seasonal=1: Has summer (su-habit, su-plumes, su-foliage) and fall (fa-color). The plant has documented winter interest ('bold multi-stem framework in winter', winter=true). Missing a winter shot of the bare multi-stem framework/silhouette that gives it winter garden structure. The plant description explicitly calls out winter as a third season of interest.
Note on seed_pods=2: su-plumes is an excellent close-up of the feathery plume structures (the defining display) and su-habit shows the full shrub covered in plumes — both well shown.

whole_plant=1: wi-habit-t.jpg is intended as the main habit shot (winter bare multi-stem form on a streambank) but is a low-resolution, somewhat distant snapshot — the tree is clearly identifiable but the multi-stem form is small in the frame, largely backlit against a river, and the image overall is soft/dated (appears to be a low-res older photo). The form is readable but 'cropped/cluttered' in the sense that the full height of the tree is not in frame. A clearer summer or fall shot of the full multi-stem tree showing its size and riparian habit would score 2.

thumb_framing=1: wi-habit-t.jpg — the smart-crop of the original Wikimedia photo places the tree stems centrally but the subject is small relative to the frame and the image resolution is noticeably lower than the other shots, making the thumb look soft/faint on retina screens. sp-catkins-t.jpg shows an excellent canopy view of catkins against blue sky, but no full-tree silhouette.

Note: seasonal=2 is well earned — wi (winter bare form + catkins), sp (early catkins on bare branches), su (leaves + green cones), fa (ripening cones + fall foliage/catkins) — all four seasons meaningfully covered.

seed_pods=1: su-seedheads shows seed sprays from a worm's-eye angle against sky — the oat-like spikelets are present but small/sparse; a closer shot isolating the seed spray detail against a neutral background would score 2. thumb_framing=1: su-seedheads-t.jpg thumb is a low-angle mountain landscape with the grass small in the lower third and sky dominating — the seed sprays are not clearly legible at thumbnail size. seasonal=1: all 3 shots are summer; plant is semi-evergreen with notable winter form (winter=true) and spring fresh-blue regrowth — need at least one winter shot showing the bleached-but-intact blue-gray clump (or spring showing the renewed steel-blue color) to reach seasonal=2.

foliage=1: both shots show flowers prominently; the deeply cut, palmate leaves are visible in the close shot background and in the structure shot but remain secondary/blurred — a dedicated shot with leaves clearly readable (shape and texture) would score 2. seasonal=1: both shots are summer; the blurb explicitly calls out 'deeply cut leaves that glow red in fall' as a season of interest — a fall foliage shot showing the crimson-red autumn color would complete the seasonal coverage and reach seasonal=2.

whole_plant=0: no shot shows the mat habit — sp-bloom is an extreme close-up of flowers filling the frame (no mat visible), sp-flower is a single flower against blurred background, su-foliage is a single flower with narrow leaves below; need a shot stepping back to show the full spreading silver-blue mat (with or without bloom) to score 2. foliage=1: su-foliage shows the needle-like gray-green mat below a centered flower but the foliage is secondary; a closer, broader shot of just the mat where leaf color, texture, and tight habit are the main subject would score 2. flowers=1: sp-flower is underexposed/dim (dark, rainy conditions) — the fringed petals are visible but image is too dark for crisp detail; sp-bloom is a tight zoom with overexposed magenta; a well-lit face-on shot of fringed flowers would score 2. bloom_in_context=1: sp-bloom is a very tight close-up of dense flowers (no mat visible, not a whole-plant-in-bloom shot); no shot shows the classic 'smothered mat' view from a gardening-useful distance — need a stepped-back spring shot of the mat covered in pink bloom to reach 2. thumb_framing=1: sp-flower-t.jpg is dark/underexposed so the flower, though centered, is not legible at small size. seasonal=1: spring + summer shots only; plant is evergreen (winter=true) with notable blue-gray winter mat — a winter shot showing the intact silver-blue mat foliage against bare ground (or snow) would reach seasonal=2.

foliage=1: all 4 shots are taken at/during bloom; the bold dark-green palmate foliage is always secondary to the flowers (blurred background or too small) — a summer shot dedicated to the foliage clump before bloom, where the large palmate leaves are the primary subject, would score 2. seasonal=1: all 4 shots are fall (bloom season); the plant produces bold dark-green palmate foliage from late spring through summer before bloom begins — a summer foliage shot would add a second season of interest and reach seasonal=2. (The 4 fall shots are excellent but redundant for seasonal coverage.)

whole_plant=0: no shot shows the compact mounding clump that defines this plant — su-bloom shows a single stem rising from a meadow (habit of the wild plant, not the garden mound), and there is no shot of the cultivated form as a 12–15 in compact mound; need a stepped-back shot of the whole plant to score 2. foliage=0: the labeled 'foliage' shot (su-foliage) is actually another flower-on-a-stem shot with debris background — the basal rosette of ferny gray-green leaves, the defining non-bloom feature of the plant, is nowhere visible across all 4 shots; need a dedicated basal foliage shot. flowers=1: su-flower is low-resolution and soft/blurry (clearly a small-sensor mobile photo at high zoom) — the pincushion structure is recognizable but not crisp or detailed enough to show the protruding stamens clearly; need a sharper face-on flower shot. front_facing=1: su-flower shows the head from slightly above, soft focus; su-foliage shows a lateral/angled view; no fully face-on crisp shot of the pincushion from directly in front. bloom_in_context=1: su-bloom is a single stem in a wild meadow — not the garden compact mound in full bloom; no shot shows the compact cultivated clump smothered in flowers the way the plant looks in a garden setting. thumb_framing=1: su-bloom-t.jpg has the flower head cut at the very top of the thumbnail with almost nothing but meadow grass below — the named subject is partially out of frame.

foliage=1: both shots are all-flowers; the fine-textured grayish-green leaves are visible along stems in fa-structure but are secondary and small — a shot where the narrow lance-like leaf shape and texture are clearly readable (e.g. a stem with foliage detail before or after bloom) would score 2. seed_pods=1: asters produce characteristic feathery white/silver seedheads (achenes with pappus) after bloom that feed sparrows and finches per the wildlife field; no seedhead shot exists — adding a late-fall/early-winter seedhead shot would score 2. seasonal=1: both shots are fall; the plant blooms Jul–Oct, meaning it has an active summer bloom phase (earlier July–August) distinct from the peak fall flush — a summer shot of early bloom or pre-bloom foliage would add a second season and reach seasonal=2.

flowers=1: su-flower shows a pair of flower tubes in a late/aging stage (the corolla is turning dark red — past its yellow prime); the key diagnostic 'paired yellow tubular flowers in leafy bracts' never appears in crisp, fresh yellow condition — need a shot of freshly-opened yellow paired flowers in their green bracts to score 2. front_facing=1: the flower shot shows aging tubes from the side; no face-on or clearly front-facing view of the fresh yellow tubular flowers. bloom_in_context=1: su-habit shows the shrub with tiny reddish flower-or-berry dots but they are too small and indistinct to serve as a blooming-shrub context shot; the close-up is of aging flowers — neither adequately shows the shrub as a yellow-flowered blooming plant; score would reach 2 with a fresh-flower close-up PLUS a wider bloom-context shot. thumb_framing=1: su-habit-t.jpg is a scanned film photo with washed-out contrast and film grain; the shrub is right-edge-heavy and the coastal background competes; a modern digital photo of the whole shrub in a cleaner garden setting would score 2. seasonal=0: all 4 shots are tagged summer; the blurb and seasons field explicitly note 'yellow fall foliage' and 'bare architectural stems in winter' as ornamental features — at least a fall foliage shot (yellow leaves) and ideally a winter stem shot are both missing; this is a multi-season shrub with zero non-summer coverage.

foliage=1: rose foliage is visible in both shots but secondary to the blooms; a shot where the compound pinnate leaves with serrated leaflets are the main subject would score 2 — useful for identification and to show typical leaf health. seed_pods=0: the blurb/seasons field explicitly calls out 'hips & bare canes in winter' as a season feature; rose hips are a notable ornamental AND wildlife feature (feeds birds) per the wildlife field; no hip shot exists at all — need a fall/winter shot of the red/orange hips on canes to score 2. seasonal=0: both shots are summer; the seasons field describes 'repeat flushes late spring–fall (by cultivar); hips & bare canes in winter' — this plant has at minimum three distinct seasonal phases (summer bloom, fall hips, winter cane structure) and only the peak summer bloom is represented; need at minimum a fall shot (hips or late-season repeat bloom) to reach seasonal=1, and fall hips + winter canes to reach seasonal=2.
